evals.expected_output: A normal-mode LifeOps daily briefing across the seven primary operating contexts (MTS Pro Services, Darkstar Technology, Family, Home, School, Parenting, Personal Operations). The briefing file is written to briefings/YYYY-MM-DD.md in the canonical section order. The skill reads compact indexes under state/indexes/ rather than performing a full repository scan. The chat surface is concise — counts plus Decisions / Waiting / Today / Upcoming lines in full.

assertions.text: Output briefing includes a 'Today' section
Output briefing includes a 'Decisions needed' section
Output briefing includes 'Waiting on me' and 'Waiting on others' sections (or affirmative empty lines)
Output briefing includes an 'Upcoming' section with at least one anchor whose lead-time ring is open per context/dates.md
Output briefing includes a 'Triage' section (or affirmative empty line if no triage items)
Output briefing includes an 'Audit and data quality' section reflecting the auditor pass, not pre-filled by the main thread
Output briefing includes an 'Activity' section reporting run mode, indexes read, source files opened, and approximate token / read budget status
Skill ran in normal daily mode — no announcement of bootstrap, reindex, or audit-only mode in chat, and the activity log records skill_mode: normal-daily

No full repo scan was performed — the activity log enumerates indexes read and skipped source files, and the count of opened source files is meaningfully smaller than the count of files under state/projects/ alone
Output is concise and executive-readable — chat surface is countable; the briefing file is the full detail

evals.expected_output: The skill triggers from natural phrasing (no explicit slash command) and produces a normal daily LifeOps briefing rather than a generic answer.

assertions.text: Skill triggers correctly from this phrasing — output is a daily-briefing run, not a freeform chat response
A briefings/YYYY-MM-DD.md file is written (or updated if already exists for today)

All canonical action sections are present (Today, Decisions needed, Waiting on me, Waiting on others, Upcoming, Triage, Approvals queue) with empty sections omitted only when absence is not meaningful
The chat surface includes the Decisions / Waiting / Today / Upcoming lines in full and a pointer to the briefing file

evals.expected_output: Normal daily mode runs and produces the briefing. The auditor subagent is invoked in fresh context against the briefing and log. The audit pass is lightweight — it reads only the cited sources and indexes, not the entire repository — and its findings are reconciled into the briefing's Audit and data quality section.

assertions.text: The auditor subagent is invoked in fresh context per .claude/agents/auditor.md, with the briefing path, log path, and cited-paths list provided as inputs
The audit pass does not regenerate the briefing or rerun normal-mode source-discovery — it reads only the cited sources plus the briefing and log
Audit and data quality section in the briefing reflects the auditor's actual findings (verdict, blocker count, warning count, highest severity, audit log path), not pre-filled content
If the auditor returns a 'block' verdict, the skill stops and surfaces the blocker rather than auto-regenerating

evals.expected_output: Skill switches to bootstrap/rebuild or reindex mode (depending on whether any indexes exist). Announces the mode in chat. Performs the full canonical scan (bootstrap) or per-category refresh (reindex). Writes the index files under state/indexes/ and a bootstrap or reindex log under logs/. Does not produce a daily briefing as a side effect unless the CEO explicitly asks for both.

assertions.text: Skill announces the mode in chat — either 'Running in bootstrap/rebuild mode — full canonical scan. Token budget exceeded by design.' or 'Running in reindex mode — refreshing <list of indexes>.'
All canonical index files exist under state/indexes/ after the run (briefing-contract.md, dates.md, waiting.md, decisions.md, approvals.md, briefing-surface.md, projects.md, triage.md)
A bootstrap or reindex log is written under logs/<date>-briefing-bootstrap.md or logs/<date>-briefing-reindex.md with skill_mode set accordingly
No briefings/YYYY-MM-DD.md is produced unless the CEO explicitly asked for both; instead the skill prompts the CEO to confirm before running normal daily mode against the new indexes

evals.expected_output: The Monday weekly review only runs when explicitly invoked or when today is Monday and the CEO explicitly confirms the deep pass. It is never triggered as a side effect of normal daily mode, even on Monday. The extended scan reads all of state/projects/, runs a confirmation pass on inferred-active items, sweeps triage ages, and produces an 'Approaching this week' rollup. Token budget for normal daily mode is not exceeded by the daily run.

assertions.text: Normal daily mode invoked on a Monday with no explicit weekly-review request does NOT run the deep pass — it remains index-first and bounded

When the CEO says 'Monday weekly review' (or 'weekly LifeOps review' / 'run the deep pass'), the skill announces 'Running Monday weekly review — extended scan.' in chat and reads beyond the eight required indexes

The weekly review surfaces a confirmation pass over any confidence: inferred items in the project shelf (e.g., the MTS inferred-active backlog), proposing promotion to confirmed or demotion to dormant
The weekly review sweeps state/indexes/triage.md for items with triage_age_days greater than seven and flags them in the Triage section of the briefing
The Activity section records skill_mode: monday-weekly-review and declares the extended-scan budget exception
